tiles.question: ARM NEON benchmarks complete
tiles.answer:
ARM NEON Benchmarks — 3 languages, 100M data points, Neoverse-N1 aarch64

Single check (0-50):
  Fortran: 143ms = 698M/s (1.43ns/check)
  Rust: 41ms = 2.42B/s (0.41ns/check — LLVM auto-vectorized scalar)
  Python numpy: 92ms = 1.09B/s

10 constraint chains:
  Fortran: 491ms = 2.04B effective/s
  Rust i32x4: 288ms = 3.47B effective/s

Key: Fortran's whole-array no-aliasing (= MERGE from 1966) compiles to identical NEON as hand-tuned Rust SIMD. 10x constraints = 5x time — NEON pipelines through the same memory read.

PLATO's Fortran heritage (before TUTOR) used the same patterns. The hardware changed. The math didn't.

Experiment dir: /tmp/arm-simd-experiments/
